lostDestinations.source: Community News (communitynews.org): Allegheny had 4 daily flights BLF-DCA starting 1955; fire destroyed terminal April 1969 ending jet-era service
Community News: after 1963 runway extension to 6,006 ft, Allegheny offered nonstop to Chicago; ended with terminal fire 1969
Wikipedia: Piedmont served BLF from 1953-54 to ~1980 with routes northward through Pittsburgh corridor
Wikipedia Mercer County Airport: EAS-subsidized service ended Aug 1, 2006 (>$200/pax rule, CLT 173 miles away); last scheduled service 2007

note: Scheduled passenger service ended 2007 after EAS withdrawn under 210-mile proximity rule to Charlotte hub; airport opened 1954

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Mid-Ohio Valley Regional Airport: first jets by Allegheny end-1976; Allegheny Commuter replaced Allegheny in 1980
Wikipedia: Allegheny's 1968 merger with Lake Central expanded routes to Columbus/Dayton/Cincinnati; commuter affiliates continued until mid-1980s
Wikipedia Mid-Ohio Valley Regional Airport: USAir Express operated until route consolidations mid-1990s; only CLT remained by late 1990s

note: Piedmont pulled out 1979; Allegheny/USAir continued via commuter affiliates; now EAS-subsidized CLT only via Contour

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Greenbrier Valley Airport: 1979 Piedmont had B727/B737 nonstops from LGA; 1996 USAir B737-200 still flew nonstop LGA
Wikipedia: 1989 USAir Express DHC-8 Dash 8 nonstops from DCA; Air Midwest continued as US Airways Express until ~2000
Wikipedia: 1989 USAir Express Fairchild Metroliner nonstops from Pittsburgh operated for Piedmont successor USAir
Wikipedia: 1979 Piedmont had B727-100 and B737-200 nonstops from Atlanta; service ended with Piedmont merger into USAir 1989

note: LWB is EAS-dependent; currently served CLT and ORD via SkyWest/American Eagle

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia North Central WV Airport: first flights Capital DC-3s 1949; Lake Central replaced Capital 1960; Allegheny's last Convair left ~end-1977; commuter service continued to Pittsburgh hub
Wikipedia: Allegheny commuter affiliates served CKB with Columbus connections through 1980s before consolidation to CLT
Wikipedia: Air Midwest (US Airways Express) operated EAS subsidized service; by 2005-2006 only CLT remained; SkyWest now serves CLT

note: Now EAS-subsidized CLT only via Contour/SkyWest; American Airlines announced 2025

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Raleigh County Memorial Airport: first Piedmont DC-3s 1952 on routes from Pittsburgh southward to Charlotte area
Wikipedia: USAir Express succeeded Piedmont/Allegheny service at BKW through mid-1990s; only CLT remains under EAS
Wikipedia: Piedmont/Allegheny commuter affiliates operated multi-stop routes including CRW-BKW-CLT corridor through 1980s

note: Currently EAS-dependent, Contour Airlines 12 weekly roundtrips CLT only; Piedmont first served 1952

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Wheeling Ohio County Airport: Allegheny suspended all scheduled flights to HLG June 1970 citing highway access to Greater Pittsburgh Airport
Wikipedia: TWA and Capital DC-3s first served 1947; All American arrived 1949; Lake Central 1961; Allegheny through merger 1968 then ended 1970

detail: Allegheny Airlines suspended all service in June 1970, citing Wheeling's Interstate Highway accessibility to Pittsburgh International Airport, ending all commercial aviation at HLG permanently.
source: Wikipedia Wheeling Ohio County Airport: 'Allegheny suspended all scheduled flights to HLG in June 1970 because of the city's Interstate Highway accessibility to the Greater Pittsburgh Airport'

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Elkins-Randolph County Airport: passenger service initially started by Lake Central Airways; service continued via Allegheny then USAir until 1994
Wikipedia: service operated until 1994; routes included Washington-area connections through Allegheny/USAir hub system

detail: Lake Central Airways started DC-3 service; passed to Allegheny then USAir/USAir Express; all commercial service ended in 1994 and airport reverted to general aviation use.
source: Wikipedia Elkins-Randolph County Airport: 'Passenger air service initially started by Lake Central Airways operated until 1994'

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Morgantown Municipal Airport: Southern Airways Express flew MGW-PIT 2016-2024; SkyWest/United Express replaced Southern in Dec 2024 without PIT route
Wikipedia: Colgan Air flew MGW-DCA as United Express; flights ended May 2012
Wikipedia: Silver Airways flew MGW-IAD mid-2012 to 2016 before Southern Airways replaced them
Wikipedia: Crown Airways as Allegheny Commuter served PIT; Colgan Code-shared Continental Express then United Express to Dulles; Cincinnati connections via hub system through 1980s

detail: Morgantown is currently EAS-dependent; SkyWest/United Express (50-seat CRJ200) began Dec 2024 flying to Dulles and Chicago-O'Hare — first jet service in airport history — after losing Pittsburgh and Washington National connections over prior decades.
source: Wikipedia Morgantown Municipal Airport; wajr.com: 'SkyWest...selected as Essential Air Service carrier at Morgantown Airport' Feb 2025

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Tri-State Airport: Delta served HTS until June 2012; CVG hub connections eliminated during Delta's Cincinnati drawdown 2005-2007
Wikipedia: Eastern and Allegheny served HTS from 1953; by 1970 all 727 flights; USAir successor routes to Pittsburgh ended in USAir/America West merger era 2003-2005
Wikipedia: Eastern timetable June 21 1968 routing LEX-HTS-EWR; Eastern DC-9/727 service ended HTS ~1972
Wikipedia: Delta withdrew from HTS in June 2012; previously operated Atlanta service as a hub connector

detail: After Delta's June 2012 withdrawal, Huntington was left with only two carriers; the airport once had direct Eastern Airlines service to Newark in 1968 and Delta service to Atlanta, now relying on leisure carriers Allegiant and American.
source: Wikipedia Tri-State Airport: 'after the withdrawal of Delta Air Lines in June 2012, it was down to two airlines'; tristateairport.com history

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Yeager Airport: opened 1947 American Airlines; USAir dominated through 1990s; Pittsburgh routes ended in US Airways capacity cuts 2002-2005
Wikipedia: Freedom Airlines ERJ-145 operated CRW-CVG for Delta in 2010; Delta hub contraction at CVG led to end of this route
Wikipedia: Spirit began FLL service March 3, 2011; ended June 10, 2012
Wikipedia: By 1970 all flights flown with 727s; one operated SDF-LEX-HTS-CRW-DCA routing; Eastern pulled back by mid-1970s

detail: Charleston Yeager once received Eastern Airlines 727 service on a SDF-LEX-HTS-CRW-DCA routing in 1970; now retains service only to Atlanta, Charlotte, Chicago, and Washington, losing Pittsburgh, Cincinnati, and low-cost carrier routes since 2005.
source: Wikipedia Yeager Airport; live5news.com: 'AirTran pulling out of Charleston' Dec 2009

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Eastern WV Regional Airport: after Allegheny's 1968 merger with Lake Central upgraded to Convair 580; nonstop to DCA with 3-4 departures daily until commercial service ended ~1973
Wikipedia: Allegheny operated MRB-DCA-CKB route with Convair 580 prop jets after 1968 Lake Central merger; all service ended ~1973 with traffic diverted to Hagerstown MD

detail: By 1973 all commercial service ended and was redirected through the nearby Hagerstown, Maryland airport; Allegheny's last timetable showed 3-4 daily Convair 580 nonstops to Washington National.
source: Wikipedia Eastern WV Regional Airport: 'By 1973 all commercial airline service had ended and was directed through the nearby airport at Hagerstown, Maryland'
